title: Chrysanthemum adenanthum
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum adenanthum belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification indicates that Chrysanthemum adenanthum shares characteristics with other members of the Asteraceae family. The genus Chrysanthemum is a group of flowering plants that are native to Asia and Europe. Within the Asteraceae family, Chrysanthemum adenanthum is likely to be a part of the subfamily Anthemidoideae, which includes annual and perennial herbs. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships between Chrysanthemum adenanthum and other related plant species.
uses: Chrysanthemum adenanthum, a lesser-known species within the Chrysanthemum genus, has been researched for its potential medicinal properties. However, its traditional uses and applications are not well-documented, and more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy. Some reports suggest that Chrysanthemum adenanthum may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, although this requires further scientific verification. Its extracts have been investigated for their potential in treating various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues. benefits: Chrysanthemum adenanthum is known for its various health benefits, primarily due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It has been traditionally used to treat fever, sore throat, and other respiratory issues. constituents: Chrysanthemum adenanthum contains various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These active constituents contribute to the plant's pharmacological properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Studies have identified the presence of compounds like adenanthin, adenanthone, and flavonoid glycosides in Chrysanthemum adenanthum. These compounds may be responsible for the plant's potential therapeutic effects, including its use in traditional medicine. parts: Chrysanthemum adenanthum is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. preparations: Chrysanthemum adenanthum, a plant traditionally used in herbal medicine, is believed to have various health benefits. side_effects: Chrysanthemum adenanthum, also known as the Asian chrysanthemum, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including treating fever and inflammation. intro: Chrysanthemum adenanthum, also known as the white-flowering chrysanthemum, is a traditional Chinese medicinal herb belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, and is traditionally used to treat various conditions such as fever, sore throat, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum adenanthum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Chrysanthemum adenanthum is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create formulas for treating a range of health conditions.
